pi_items.source: setup_opentoonz.sh downloads the opentoonz_sample repo to /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/, which includes dwanko_run.tnz
setup_task.sh creates this directory and export_result.sh checks it for output files.
verifier.py requires at least 24 frames for full score and export_result.sh counts them.
export_result.sh explicitly filters and counts files matching *.png.
Code analysis reveals verifier.py does not evaluate file sizes or enforce this minimum size constraint. export_result.sh exports total_size_bytes but it is unused.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real 'dwanko_run.tnz' sample scene downloaded from the official OpenToonz GitHub repository. The agent must render an animation with an underwater distortion effect to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/underwater_fx/'. The verifier strictly checks that at least 24 new PNG files are created during the task. It also uses a VLM to visually confirm that a character is visible and an underwater (wavy/distorted) effect has been applied to a sample frame. No minimum file size is enforced.
